---
title: "ABitOfMe_UMAP26"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-30"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

```{r libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(glmnet) 
library(caret) 
library(simr) 
library(lsa) 
library(lme4) 
library(lmerTest) 
library(MASS)
library(car)
library(broom.mixed)
library(scales)
library(MuMIn)
library(glmmLasso)
```

```{r load_data}
#only inclucde receivers who wrote at least 60 words
data <- read_csv("data.csv")
data <- data |> filter(WC_receiver >= 60) 

```

```{r GLM}
data <- read_csv("data.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  filter(WC_receiver >= 60)

model_data <- data %>%
  dplyr::select(persuasion_score, receiverID, statementID)

#predictor sets below:

#LIWC
liwc_statement <- names(data %>% dplyr::select(Segment_statement:Emoji_statement))
liwc_receiver <- names(data %>% dplyr::select(Segment_receiver:Emoji_receiver))
  
#CONTENT
content_statement <- names(data %>% dplyr::select(Questions_statement:alternatives_statement))
content_receiver <- names(data %>% dplyr::select(Questions_receiver:alternatives_receiver)) 
  
#PERSONALITY
personality_statement <- names(data %>% dplyr::select(extraversion_statement:neuroticism_statement))
personality_receiver <- names(data %>% dplyr::select(extraversion_receiver:neuroticism_receiver))
  
#DEMOGRAPHICS
demographics_receiver <- names(data %>% dplyr::select(gender_receiver, age_receiver, educationlevel_receiver))
demographics_statement <- names(data %>% dplyr::select(gender_statement, age_statement, educationlevel_statement))

x_cols <- personality_statement
#choose any from above sets of predictors

predictors <- data %>% dplyr::select(all_of(x_cols)) 

# keep only nonzero-variance predictors
vars <- vapply(predictors, \(x) var(x, na.rm = TRUE), numeric(1))
nonzero_vars <- names(vars)[is.finite(vars) & vars > 0]

predictors_scaled <- predictors %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(nonzero_vars)) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ as.numeric(scale(.))))

model_data <- bind_cols(model_data, predictors_scaled)

model_data_glm <- model_data %>%
  dplyr::select(-receiverID, -statementID) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  as.data.frame()

glm_full <- glm(persuasion_score ~ ., data = model_data_glm)

#function to remove aliased and high-VIF predictors
remove_alias_and_high_vif_vars <- function(data, response = "persuasion_score", vars, cutoff = 5) {
  vars <- setdiff(vars, response)

  repeat {
    fml <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", paste(vars, collapse = " + ")))
    m <- glm(fml, data = data)

    #remove aliased columns (there were some in liwc_statement)
    aliased <- setdiff(names(coef(m))[is.na(coef(m))], "(Intercept)")
    if (length(aliased) > 0) {
      drop_var <- intersect(aliased, vars)[1]
      if (is.na(drop_var)) drop_var <- vars[1]
#     cat("Removing aliased:", drop_var, "\n")
      vars <- setdiff(vars, drop_var)
      next
    }

    #get VIF, remove any NA VIF first
    v <- car::vif(m)
    if (is.matrix(v)) v <- v[, 1]
    if (anyNA(v)) {
      drop_var <- names(v)[which(is.na(v))[1]]
#     cat("Removing NA VIF:", drop_var, "\n")
      vars <- setdiff(vars, drop_var)
      next
    }

    if (all(v < cutoff)) break

    #remove high VIF
    high_vif <- names(which.max(v))
#   cat("Removing high VIF:", high_vif, "(VIF =", round(max(v), 2), ")\n")
    vars <- setdiff(vars, high_vif)
  }

  vars
}

#apply to model
model_data_glm <- as.data.frame(model_data_glm)
vars0 <- setdiff(names(model_data_glm), "persuasion_score")
vars_kept <- remove_alias_and_high_vif_vars(model_data_glm, vars = vars0, cutoff = 5)

glm_vif_filtered <- glm(
  as.formula(paste("persuasion_score ~", paste(vars_kept, collapse = " + "))),
  data = model_data_glm
)

#stepwise model on remaining predictors
stepwise_model <- step(glm_vif_filtered, direction = "both", trace = TRUE)

#refit glm with remaining predictors
fixed_part <- paste(names(coef(stepwise_model))[-1], collapse = " + ")
glm_formula <- if (nzchar(fixed_part)) as.formula(paste("persuasion_score ~", fixed_part)) else persuasion_score ~ 1

final_model <- glm(glm_formula, data = model_data_glm)
summary(final_model)

#pseudo-Rsq
r_squared <- 1 - (final_model$deviance / final_model$null.deviance)
r_squared

# Likelihood ratio test
null_model <- glm(persuasion_score ~ 1, data = model_data_glm)
anova(null_model, final_model, test = "Chisq")



```

```{r mixed_lasso}

set.seed(123)

#re-load data if re-running
data <- read_csv("data.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  filter(WC_receiver >= 60)

#predictor sets below:

#LIWC
liwc_statement <- names(data %>% dplyr::select(Segment_statement:Emoji_statement))
liwc_receiver <- names(data %>% dplyr::select(Segment_receiver:Emoji_receiver))
  
#CONTENT
content_statement <- names(data %>% dplyr::select(Questions_statement:alternatives_statement))
content_receiver <- names(data %>% dplyr::select(Questions_receiver:alternatives_receiver)) 
  
#PERSONALITY
personality_statement <- names(data %>% dplyr::select(extraversion_statement:neuroticism_statement))
personality_receiver <- names(data %>% dplyr::select(extraversion_receiver:neuroticism_receiver))
  
#DEMOGRAPHICS
demographics_statement <- names(data %>% dplyr::select(gender_receiver, age_receiver, educationlevel_receiver))
demographics_receiver <- names(data %>% dplyr::select(gender_statement, age_statement, educationlevel_statement))


predictors <- liwc_receiver #choose any from above sets of predictors


data <- data %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(predictors), persuasion_score, receiverID, statementID) %>%
  mutate(
    receiverID = as.factor(receiverID),
    statementID = as.factor(statementID)
  ) %>%
  filter(complete.cases(.)) %>%
  as.data.frame()

# drop zero-variance predictors and scale
sds <- vapply(data[, predictors, drop = FALSE], sd, numeric(1), na.rm = TRUE)
predictors <- predictors[is.finite(sds) & sds > 0]

data[, predictors] <- lapply(data[, predictors, drop = FALSE], \(x) as.numeric(scale(x)))


#lmer formula
random_list <- list(receiverID = ~1, statementID = ~1)
fixed_formula <- as.formula(paste("persuasion_score ~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + ")))

#function to choose best lambda to minimise AIC 
lambda_grid <- 10^seq(from = -2, to = 3, length.out = 16) # 16 equally spaced lambda values on a log scale 
aic_tbl <- data.frame(lambda = lambda_grid, n_selected = NA_integer_, AIC_lmer = NA_real_)
lasso_models <- vector("list", length(lambda_grid))

for (i in seq_along(lambda_grid)) {
  lam <- lambda_grid[i]
#  cat("Lambda:", lam, "\n")

  lasso <- tryCatch(
    glmmLasso(
      fixed_formula,
      rnd = random_list,
      data = data,
      lambda = lam,
      family = gaussian(link = "identity")
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  lasso_models[[i]] <- lasso
  if (is.null(lasso)) next

  selected <- setdiff(names(lasso$coeff)[lasso$coeff != 0], "(Intercept)")
#  aic_tbl$n_selected[i] <- length(selected)

  fml <- if (length(selected) == 0) {
    persuasion_score ~ 1 + (1 | receiverID) + (1 | statementID)
  } else {
    as.formula(paste(
      "persuasion_score ~", paste(selected, collapse = " + "),
      "+ (1 | receiverID) + (1 | statementID)"
    ))
  }

  lmer_fit <- tryCatch(lmer(fml, data = data, REML = FALSE), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (!is.null(lmer_fit)) aic_tbl$AIC_lmer[i] <- AIC(lmer_fit)
}

aic_tbl <- aic_tbl[order(aic_tbl$AIC_lmer), ]
print(aic_tbl)

valid <- which(is.finite(aic_tbl$AIC_lmer))
best_i <- valid[which.min(aic_tbl$AIC_lmer[valid])] #select index of min AIC
best_lambda <- aic_tbl$lambda[best_i] #and the corresponding lambda
cat("Best lambda (lmer AIC):", best_lambda, "\n")


#fit lasso with best lambda
glmm_lasso_model <- lasso_models[[best_i]]
if (is.null(glmm_lasso_model)) {
  glmm_lasso_model <- glmmLasso(
    fixed_formula,
    rnd = random_list,
    data = data,
    lambda = best_lambda,
    family = gaussian(link = "identity")
  )
}

#fit lmer with remaining predictors (to get estimates, pvalues etc)
selected_vars <- setdiff(names(glmm_lasso_model$coeff)[glmm_lasso_model$coeff != 0], "(Intercept)")
#print(selected_vars)

final_formula <- if (length(selected_vars) == 0) {
  persuasion_score ~ 1 + (1 | receiverID) + (1 | statementID)
} else {
  as.formula(paste(
    "persuasion_score ~", paste(selected_vars, collapse = " + "),
    "+ (1 | receiverID) + (1 | statementID)"
  ))
}

final_lmer_ml <- lmer(final_formula, data = data, REML = FALSE)
final_lmer <- update(final_lmer_ml, REML = TRUE)
summary(final_lmer)
# 10-fold cross-validated RMSE
k <- 10
folds <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = nrow(data)))
rmse_vec <- numeric(k)
for (i in 1:k) {
  train_data <- data[folds != i, , drop = FALSE]
  test_data  <- data[folds == i, , drop = FALSE]
  m <- lmer(final_formula, data = train_data, REML = TRUE)
  preds <- predict(m, newdata = test_data, allow.new.levels = TRUE)
  rmse_vec[i] <- sqrt(mean((test_data$persuasion_score - preds)^2))
}
cv_rmse <- mean(rmse_vec)
cv_rmse

#lmer results
r2 <- r.squaredGLMM(final_lmer)
r2

summary(final_lmer)
AIC(final_lmer)

#likelihood ratio test
null_lmer_ml <- lmer(persuasion_score ~ 1 + (1 | receiverID) + (1 | statementID), data = data, REML = FALSE)
anova(null_lmer_ml, final_lmer_ml)
AIC(null_lmer_ml); AIC(final_lmer_ml)



```


```{r similarity_df}
data <- read_csv("data.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  filter(WC_receiver >= 60)

#all features
features <- c(
  "Segment", "WC", "Analytic", "Clout", "Authentic", "Tone",
  "WPS", "BigWords", "Dic", "function", "pronoun", "ppron",
  "i", "we", "you", "shehe", "they", "ipron", "det", "article",
  "number", "prep", "auxverb", "adverb", "conj", "negate",
  "verb", "adj", "quantity", "Drives", "affiliation", "achieve",
  "power", "Cognition", "allnone", "cogproc", "insight", "cause",
  "discrep", "tentat", "certitude", "differ", "memory", "Affect",
  "tone_pos", "tone_neg", "emotion", "emo_pos", "emo_neg",
  "emo_anx", "emo_anger", "emo_sad", "swear", "Social",
  "socbehav", "prosocial", "polite", "conflict", "moral",
  "comm", "socrefs", "family", "friend", "female", "male",
  "Culture", "politic", "ethnicity", "tech", "Lifestyle",
  "leisure", "home", "work", "money", "relig", "Physical",
  "health", "illness", "wellness", "mental", "substances",
  "sexual", "food", "death", "need", "want", "acquire",
  "lack", "fulfill", "fatigue", "reward", "risk",
  "curiosity", "allure", "Perception", "attention", "motion",
  "space", "visual", "auditory", "feeling", "time",
  "focuspast", "focuspresent", "focusfuture", "Conversation",
  "netspeak", "assent", "nonflu", "filler", "AllPunc",
  "Period", "Comma", "QMark", "Exclam", "Apostro",
  "OtherP", "Emoji",
  "Questions", "Social_pressure", "Pleasant_Experience",
  "Health_reasons", "Financial_reasons", "Ethical_reasons",
  "Environmental_reasons", "Easy_to_do", "Praise",
  "Offers_to_Help", "Criticism", "Barriers",
  "Start_small", "Practical_steps", "alternatives", "extraversion",
  "agreeableness", "conscientiousness", "neuroticism",
  "openness", "age", "gender", "educationlevel"
)

author_cols <- paste0(features, "_statement")
receiver_cols  <- paste0(features, "_receiver")

data[author_cols] <- lapply(data[author_cols], function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x)))
data[receiver_cols]  <- lapply(data[receiver_cols], function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x)))

#initialise similarity dataframe
similarity_df <- data %>% dplyr::select(statementID, receiverID, persuasion_score)

#compute similarity feature-by-feature
for (feat in features) {
  author_col <- paste0(feat, "_statement")
  receiver_col  <- paste0(feat, "_receiver")
  
  similarity_df[[paste0("sim_", feat)]] <- 1 - abs(data[[receiver_col]] - data[[author_col]]) / 
    pmax(abs(data[[receiver_col]]), abs(data[[author_col]]), 1e-6)
}

write_csv(similarity_df, "similarity_df.csv")

similarity_df <- similarity_df %>% na.omit()



```

```{r GLM_similarity}
similarity_df <- read_csv("similarity_df.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

model_data <- similarity_df %>%
  dplyr::select(persuasion_score, receiverID, statementID)

#predictor sets
liwc_content_similarity <- similarity_df%>% dplyr::select(sim_Segment:sim_Practical_steps)
personality_similarity <- similarity_df %>% dplyr::select(sim_extraversion:sim_openness)
demographics_similarity <- similarity_df %>% dplyr::select(sim_gender, sim_age, sim_educationlevel)
combined <- similarity_df %>% dplyr::select(sim_Segment:sim_Practical_steps, sim_extraversion:sim_openness, sim_gender, sim_age, sim_educationlevel)

predictors <-  demographics_similarity#choose any of the sets

# keep only nonzero-variance predictors
vars <- vapply(predictors, \(x) var(x, na.rm = TRUE), numeric(1))
nonzero_vars <- names(vars)[is.finite(vars) & vars > 0]

#scale
predictors_scaled <- predictors %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(nonzero_vars)) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ as.numeric(scale(.))))

model_data <- bind_cols(model_data, predictors_scaled)

model_data_glm <- model_data %>%
  dplyr::select(-receiverID, -statementID) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  as.data.frame()

glm_full <- glm(persuasion_score ~ ., data = model_data_glm)

#remove aliased and high-vif predictors
remove_alias_and_high_vif_vars <- function(data, response = "persuasion_score", vars, cutoff = 5) {
  vars <- setdiff(vars, response)

  repeat {
    fml <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", paste(vars, collapse = " + ")))
    m <- glm(fml, data = data)

    aliased <- setdiff(names(coef(m))[is.na(coef(m))], "(Intercept)")
    if (length(aliased) > 0) {
      drop_var <- intersect(aliased, vars)[1]
      if (is.na(drop_var)) drop_var <- vars[1]
      cat("Removing aliased:", drop_var, "\n")
      vars <- setdiff(vars, drop_var)
      next
    }

    v <- car::vif(m)
    if (is.matrix(v)) v <- v[, 1]
    if (anyNA(v)) {
      drop_var <- names(v)[which(is.na(v))[1]]
      cat("Removing NA VIF:", drop_var, "\n")
      vars <- setdiff(vars, drop_var)
      next
    }

    if (all(v < cutoff)) break

    worst <- names(which.max(v))
    cat("Removing high VIF:", worst, "(VIF =", round(max(v), 2), ")\n")
    vars <- setdiff(vars, worst)
  }

  vars
}

model_data_glm <- as.data.frame(model_data_glm)

#refit model with remaining predictors and run stepwise selection
vars0 <- setdiff(names(model_data_glm), "persuasion_score")
vars_kept <- remove_alias_and_high_vif_vars(model_data_glm, vars = vars0, cutoff = 5)

glm_vif_filtered <- glm(
  as.formula(paste("persuasion_score ~", paste(vars_kept, collapse = " + "))),
  data = model_data_glm
)

stepwise_model <- step(glm_vif_filtered, direction = "both", trace = TRUE)


# final GLM from selected terms
fixed_part <- paste(names(coef(stepwise_model))[-1], collapse = " + ")
glm_formula <- if (nzchar(fixed_part)) as.formula(paste("persuasion_score ~", fixed_part)) else persuasion_score ~ 1

final_model <- glm(glm_formula, data = model_data_glm)
summary(final_model)

#pseudo Rsq
r_squared <- 1 - (final_model$deviance / final_model$null.deviance)
r_squared

#likelihood ratio test
null_model <- glm(persuasion_score ~ 1, data = model_data_glm)
anova(null_model, final_model, test = "Chisq")


```

```{r lasso_similarity}

#reload data (if running more than once)
similarity_df <- read_csv("similarity_df.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

set.seed(123)

#sets of similarity predictors
liwc_cont_sim <- names(similarity_df |> dplyr::select(sim_Segment:sim_Practical_steps)) #similarity liwc+content
pers_sim <- names(similarity_df |> dplyr::select(sim_extraversion:sim_openness)) #similarity personality
dem_sim <- names(similarity_df |> dplyr::select(sim_gender, sim_age, sim_educationlevel)) #similarity demographics
combined <- names(similarity_df |> dplyr::select(sim_Segment:sim_Practical_steps, sim_extraversion:sim_openness, sim_gender, sim_age, sim_educationlevel)) #all similarity predictors

predictors <- pers_sim

#choose best lambda from a sequence on a log scale
lambda_grid <- 10^seq(from = -2, to = 3, length.out = 16)

#initialise data and random effects
similarity_df <- similarity_df %>%
  dplyr::select(persuasion_score, receiverID, statementID, all_of(predictors)) %>%
  mutate(
    receiverID = as.factor(receiverID),
    statementID = as.factor(statementID),
    across(all_of(predictors), ~ as.numeric(as.character(.)))
  ) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  as.data.frame()

# drop zero-variance predictors and scale
sds <- vapply(similarity_df[, predictors, drop = FALSE], sd, numeric(1), na.rm = TRUE)
predictors <- predictors[is.finite(sds) & sds > 0]

similarity_df[, predictors] <-
  lapply(similarity_df[, predictors, drop = FALSE], \(x) as.numeric(scale(x)))

# model formula
random_list <- list(receiverID = ~1, statementID = ~1)
fixed_formula <- as.formula(paste("persuasion_score ~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + ")))

#function to choose best lambda minimising AIC
aic_tbl <- data.frame(lambda = lambda_grid, n_selected = NA_integer_, AIC_lmer = NA_real_)
lasso_models <- vector("list", length(lambda_grid))


for (i in seq_along(lambda_grid)) {
  lam <- lambda_grid[i]

  lasso <- tryCatch(
    glmmLasso(
      fixed_formula,
      rnd = random_list,
      data = similarity_df,
      lambda = lam,
      family = gaussian(link = "identity")
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  lasso_models[[i]] <- lasso
  if (is.null(lasso)) next

  selected <- names(lasso$coeff)[lasso$coeff != 0]
  selected <- setdiff(selected, "(Intercept)")
  aic_tbl$n_selected[i] <- length(selected)

  fml <- if (length(selected) == 0) {
    persuasion_score ~ 1 + (1 | receiverID) + (1 | statementID)
  } else {
    as.formula(paste(
      "persuasion_score ~", paste(selected, collapse = " + "),
      "+ (1 | receiverID) + (1 | statementID)"
    ))
  }

  lmer_fit <- tryCatch(
    lmer(fml, data = similarity_df, REML = FALSE),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (is.null(lmer_fit)) next

  aic_tbl$AIC_lmer[i] <- AIC(lmer_fit)
}

aic_tbl <- aic_tbl[order(aic_tbl$AIC_lmer), ]




valid <- which(is.finite(aic_tbl$AIC_lmer))
best_i <- valid[which.min(aic_tbl$AIC_lmer[valid])] #select min AIC
best_lambda <- aic_tbl$lambda[best_i] 
cat("Best lambda (lmer AIC):", best_lambda, "\n")

#fit lasso with best lambda
glmm_lasso_model <- lasso_models[[best_i]]
if (is.null(glmm_lasso_model)) {
  glmm_lasso_model <- glmmLasso(
    fixed_formula,
    rnd = random_list,
    data = similarity_df,
    lambda = best_lambda,
    family = gaussian(link = "identity")
  )
}

#refit lmer with leftover variables (to get estimates, pvalues etc)

selected_vars <- names(glmm_lasso_model$coeff)[glmm_lasso_model$coeff != 0]
selected_vars <- setdiff(selected_vars, "(Intercept)")
#cat("Selected predictors:", length(selected_vars), "\n")
#print(selected_vars)
final_formula <- if (length(selected_vars) == 0) {
  persuasion_score ~ 1 + (1 | receiverID) + (1 | statementID)
} else {
  as.formula(paste(
    "persuasion_score ~", paste(selected_vars, collapse = " + "),
    "+ (1 | receiverID) + (1 | statementID)"
  ))
}


#final model results
final_ml   <- lmer(final_formula, data = similarity_df, REML = FALSE)

summary(final_ml)
MuMIn::r.squaredGLMM(final_ml)
AIC(final_ml)


#10-fold cross-validated RMSE
k <- 10
n <- nrow(similarity_df)
folds <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = n))
rmse_vec <- numeric(k)

for (fold in 1:k) {
  train_data <- similarity_df[folds != fold, , drop = FALSE]
  test_data  <- similarity_df[folds == fold, , drop = FALSE]
  m <- lmer(final_formula, data = train_data, REML = FALSE)
  preds <- predict(m, newdata = test_data, allow.new.levels = TRUE)
  rmse_vec[fold] <- sqrt(mean((test_data$persuasion_score - preds)^2))
}

cv_rmse <- mean(rmse_vec)
cv_rmse


#likelihood ratio test
null_ml <- lmer(persuasion_score ~ 1 + (1 | receiverID) + (1 | statementID),
                data = similarity_df, REML = FALSE)
anova(null_ml, final_ml)
AIC(null_ml); AIC(final_ml)



```
